name: Guidance (Structured LLM Output Control)

auth.notes: Provider API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, local via llama.cpp/transformers)

performance.notes: Token-efficient via constrained sampling; faster than re-prompting for structure

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft Research's LLM control framework. Constrains generation via templates: lm + 'The capital of France is ' + gen('city', regex=r'[A-Z][a-z]+') + '.'. select() for choices. gen() for free generation. json() for structured JSON. Regex constraints on generation. Works with local models (llama.cpp, transformers) and API models (OpenAI). Lower-level than instructor/outlines — template-based rather than Pydantic. Token efficiency via constrained decoding. Used in Microsoft products.

gotchas: Template-based API has steep learning curve vs Pydantic (instructor/outlines)
API consistency between local and cloud models varies
Less actively developed than instructor or outlines
best_when: Fine-grained control over LLM generation with regex constraints
Local model constrained decoding (token-efficient vs prompting)
avoid_when: Type-safe structured output (instructor with Pydantic is cleaner)
summary: Guidance provides template-based LLM control with constrained generation. lm + 'City: ' + gen('city', regex=r'[A-Z]+') — enforces regex patterns during token generation. json() for structured JSON. Works with local models (llama.cpp) and API providers. Token-efficient via constrained decoding. Microsoft Research. Less ergonomic than instructor (Pydantic) but lower-level control. Useful for research and local model structured output.
